content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord Weekly Report (Apr 5 - 11, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
Development for the week of April 5–11, 2026, centered on expanding the functional scope of the Discord plugin and refining configuration management. The primary focus was achieving feature parity with OpenClaw to enhance agent capabilities, alongside targeted improvements to user ID handling. These updates reflect a continued commitment to modularity and robust configuration control within the ElizaOS ecosystem.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Feature Parity and Capability Expansion
The project took a significant step toward feature parity with OpenClaw through the introduction of PR [#46](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/pull/46). This comprehensive update aims to integrate advanced Discord interactions, including:
*   **Enhanced Interaction:** Support for typing indicators and reactions.
*   **Communication:** Implementation of streaming capabilities.
*   **Command Handling:** Introduction of slash command support.

### Configuration Management
Efforts were made to improve the reliability of environment-based settings. PR [#47](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/pull/47) was opened to address configuration logic, specifically ensuring that the system correctly respects the `MILADY_DISCORD_OWNER_USER_IDS_JSON` setting. This fix is intended to provide more precise control over Discord owner user IDs.

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
*   **Closed Issues:** No issues were closed during this reporting period.
*   **New & Active Issues:** No new issues were opened, and there are currently no active issues tracked in the provided reports. The development focus remains on the progression and review of the aforementioned pull requests.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
Development activity during this period was characterized by the submission of two significant pull requests. Both [#46](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/pull/46) and [#47](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-discord/pull/47) are currently in the review phase, indicating an active focus on code quality and integration as the plugin evolves to meet its feature parity goals.
